title: Louvre President Christophe Leribault: 'I am not here to undo the work of my predecessors'
subhed: In an interview, the successor to Laurence des Cars as head of the museum explains how he intends to make his mark with humility, courage and ambition.

title: Trump says pausing 'Project Freedom' Hormuz operation in push for Iran deal
Zelensky condemns Russia's deadly strikes in Ukraine as Moscow seeks peace for May 9 parade
'With the withdrawal of American troops from Germany, Berlin is facing a historic dilemma'
subhed: Donald Trump's announcement of the withdrawal of US troops is forcing Berlin to confront the long-standing taboo of its own defense, explains the specialist in Franco-German relations Paul Maurice for Le Monde.

title: Chinese artist Gao Zhen and his family stuck in repressive legal limbo over his Mao sculptures
subhed: Authorities are accusing the visual artist, a US resident, over works created in the 2000s that explicitly criticized Chairman Mao's regime. His wife holds out faint hope that Donald Trump's upcoming visit to Beijing, on May 14 and 15, can help her husband's case.

title: Arsenal's 20-year wait to reach Champions League final
subhed: Bukayo Saka sealed a 1-0 win against Atlético Madrid on Tuesday to bring Mikel Arteta's team to the Champions League for the first time in 20 years. Arsenal will now face Paris Saint-Germain or Bayern Munich in the final on May 30 in Budapest.

title: Military personnel at French presidency suspected of compromising classified documents
subhed: Prosecutors are investigating several members of the military and civilians suspected of setting up a private company to sell classified information and weapons of war. Six suspects have been charged.

Jean-Luc Mélenchon announces fourth presidential run
subhed: Despite the emergence of several figures within La France Insoumise and his promise to step aside after the 2022 election, in which he gathered 21.95% of the vote, the populist leader announced his candidacy on Sunday.

title: Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'The United States has lost its moral authority for good'
subhed: The United States is turning its back on democracy and freedom – values the nation once embodied – and is now replacing them with the law of the strongest and a culture of deal-making. Donald Trump has caused irreparable harm to the world's leading power, argues the economist in his column.

title: Saint Pierre and Miquelon, a pioneering example of climate change adaptation
subhed: This year, residents of a town in the French overseas territory off the coast of Canada are set to move into a new village. This relocation, France's first planned effort to move an entire town, is the result of a lengthy process.
